prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.08) and high seasonality variance (0.86), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while rf excels at trend tracking; early horizons favor ETS/THETA while late horizons benefit from DWT_svr/rf. The combination of strong disagreement signals and divergent seasonal capture strongly supports dba_combination with mixed weighting to leverage each model's unique strengths across different forecast dimensions.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "DWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "FT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "rf", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.0804,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.08) and high seasonality variance (0.86), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while rf excels at trend tracking; early horizons favor ETS/THETA while late horizons benefit from DWT_svr/rf. The combination of strong disagreement signals and divergent seasonal capture strongly supports dba_combination with mixed weighting to leverage each model's unique strengths across different forecast dimensions."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon and topk_mean. RMSE spread_ratio=1.08 shows significant dispersion between THETA (2136.73) and worst models (~4988), supporting dba_combination for phase-shift robustness as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 reduce overfitting risk. EWA with eta=1.0 provides adaptive weighting to leverage trend champions (rf) and seasonality champions (THETA). Debate not forced as candidates span different method types with distinct strengths."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin top2 is 0.0168 indicating competitive performance. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k6/k8 and inverse_rmse_weights_k8 variants but current set already has diverse types. No pruning needed as no single type dominates (max 2 of any type).",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.08) and high seasonality variance (0.86), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while rf excels at trend tracking; early horizons favor ETS/THETA while late horizons benefit from DWT_svr/rf. The combination of strong disagreement signals and divergent seasonal capture strongly supports dba_combination with mixed weighting to leverage each model's unique strengths across different forecast dimensions.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon and topk_mean. RMSE spread_ratio=1.08 shows significant dispersion between THETA (2136.73) and worst models (~4988), supporting dba_combination for phase-shift robustness as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 reduce overfitting risk. EWA with eta=1.0 provides adaptive weighting to leverage trend champions (rf) and seasonality champions (THETA). Debate not forced as candidates span different method types with distinct strengths.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin top2 is 0.0168 indicating competitive performance. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k6/k8 and inverse_rmse_weights_k8 variants but current set already has diverse types. No pruning needed as no single type dominates (max 2 of any type).
